title: The light that still shines over Ladismith
description: LADISMITH NEWS - High on the cliffs of...
bodyText: [{"type":"paragraph","text":"LADISMITH NEWS - High on the cliffs of Elandsberg above Ladismith, a small light has burned for more than 60 years, a tribute to one man’s ingenuity, endurance, and love for his town.","html":"LADISMITH NEWS - High on the cliffs of Elandsberg above Ladismith, a small light has burned for more than 60 years, a tribute to one man’s ingenuity, endurance, and love for his town."},{"type":"blockquote","text":"On 31 May 1963, Stanley de Wit (7 January 1926 – 14 October 2012), a passionate mountaineer, installed a light at the turning point of the Elandsberg hiking trail."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"This spot is 6,1 km up and 1 430 metres above sea level. The idea came to him during one of his many climbs. During these hikes, he noticed a constant stream of water cascading down a rock face.","html":"This spot is 6,1 km up and 1 430 metres above sea level. The idea came to him during one of his many climbs. During these hikes, he noticed a constant stream of water cascading down a rock face."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Using this stream, he built what the University of Cape Town’s Engineering Department later described as “probably the smallest hydro-electric unit in the world.” This light burns permanently and can be seen from town.","html":"Using this stream, he built what the University of Cape Town’s Engineering Department later described as “probably the smallest hydro-electric unit in the world.” This light burns permanently and can be seen from town."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Stanley’s handmade system powered a bicycle light visible from town at night. For 30 years, he maintained it faithfully. He made 278 climbs, more than 3 000 kilometres in total, to replace bulbs and repair the dynamo.","html":"Stanley’s handmade system powered a bicycle light visible from town at night. For 30 years, he maintained it faithfully. He made 278 climbs, more than 3 000 kilometres in total, to replace bulbs and repair the dynamo."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"When Stanley’s legs finally told him to stop, volunteers took over the upkeep of the light.","html":"When Stanley’s legs finally told him to stop, volunteers took over the upkeep of the light."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Jackie du Toit cared for it until knee problems forced him to step down, followed by Jan Barnard from Nature Conservation, who made some design improvements.","html":"Jackie du Toit cared for it until knee problems forced him to step down, followed by Jan Barnard from Nature Conservation, who made some design improvements."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Later, Hugh Sussens installed a photovoltaic system to help restart the alternator after droughts, and eventually, a solar-powered LED light replaced the original dynamo setup.","html":"Later, Hugh Sussens installed a photovoltaic system to help restart the alternator after droughts, and eventually, a solar-powered LED light replaced the original dynamo setup."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"Today, Stanley's light still shines from Elandsberg, a lasting symbol of Stanley’s dedication and the community’s commitment to keeping his legacy alive.","html":"Today, Stanley's light still shines from Elandsberg, a lasting symbol of Stanley’s dedication and the community’s commitment to keeping his legacy alive."},{"type":"paragraph","text":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’","html":"‘We bring you the latest Garden Route, Hessequa, Karoo news’"}]

images.alt: The light that still shines over Ladismith
